step.title: Discovery and Context
step.goal: Understand what skill the user wants to create, which agent will own it, and gather any existing context (architecture documents, similar skills).

substep.title: Gather Requirements
substep.ask: What skill would you like to create? Please describe: - What does this skill do? (what problem does it solve?) - Which agent(s) will invoke this skill? (e.g., ticketsmith, codesmith, prompter) - Is there a workflow spec, architecture document, or existing skill to reference?

check.action: Note any templates, scripts, or references specified
Identify integration points with other skills
Review the similar skill's structure as reference: - What type is it? (workflow, exec, data) - How many steps does it have? - What scripts/templates does it use?
Conduct lightweight discovery to understand: - Core purpose (what output does it produce?) - User interaction (multi-step or single-shot?) - Dependencies (what files/tools does it need?)

substep.title: Confirm Understanding
substep.template-output:
Summarize what you've learned: - Skill purpose - Owner agent - Expected outputs - Any constraints or requirements
Present to user for confirmation before proceeding.
[a] Explore more [c] Continue [y] YOLO

section.title: Good Discovery Questions
section.example: "What's the primary output this skill produces?" "Is this a guided process or a quick action?" "What existing files or templates should it use?" "Who will trigger this skill? (user via menu, or another skill)"
"What color should the output be?" (irrelevant) "What's the exact file structure?" (too detailed for discovery)

section.title: Skill Location and Reuse
section.description: All skills live at .vera/skills/{skill_id}/ — flat namespace. Skills are referenced by their id from any agent's permission.skill map or from another skill's invoke-skill calls. Cross-agent reuse is the criterion for skill existence; agent-internal references stay inside the agent's directory.

rules.rule: Skill should serve a clear purpose that crosses agents OR represents progressive disclosure for a multi-capability agent
Single-agent single-use logic belongs in the agent's instructions, not in a separate skill
Reusability across agents is the strongest justification

section.example: ticket-grill: used by ticketsmith and could be reused by future grill-adopting agents context-md-format: cross-agent data reference agent-creator: used by prompter

section.title: Architecture Document Integration
section.description: If an Architect agent has produced a system architecture, it may contain: - Skill definitions with inputs/outputs - Step outlines for workflow skills - Template requirements - Integration patterns
section.tip: Use architecture docs as primary source of truth. Don't re-ask questions that the architecture already answers.
